channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania partners with South Korean firms to advance Vision 2050 industrialisation goals
item.description: South Korean enterprises are positioning themselves as important partners in supporting Tanzania’s Vision 2050 development agenda, which aims to turn the country into a semi-industrialised, middle-inc...

item.title: DR Congo Health Officials Search for Ebola Patients After Fire at Treatment Centre
item.description: Health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo are carrying out an urgent operation to trace at least 20 Ebola patients who escaped from a treatment centre after a fire broke out at the facili...

item.title: BINI to Perform “Unang Kilig” Production Number on ASAP XP
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios announced that the popular P-pop girl group BINI will headline a special production number titled “Unang Kilig” on ASAP XP. The article mainly serves as a promotional entertainment upd...

item.title: Barbara Kay argues anti-Zionist narratives distort Jewish trauma and Holocaust remembrance
item.description: In this opinion piece published in the National Post, columnist Barbara Kay argues that some anti-Zionist activists and intellectuals rely on distorted narratives and selective interpretations to dele...

item.title: Suspect killed after gunfire exchange near White House checkpoint
item.description: A shooting incident occurred near the White House on Saturday evening when a man opened fire at a security checkpoint, prompting a response from United States Secret Service officers. According to an ...

item.title: Baguio records 800 HIV cases amid rising infections in Cordillera region
item.description: Health officials in Baguio City and the Cordillera region have raised concern over the continuous rise in HIV-AIDS cases, with the city now recording 800 confirmed cases. According to the Department o...

item.title: NCAA Restricts Services to 11 Nigerian Airlines Over Outstanding Financial Obligations
item.description: The Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) has directed its departments and regional offices to suspend certain services to 11 domestic airlines over unpaid financial obligations owed to the regulat...

item.title: Kerala Minister vows strict action in Sabarimala gold loss case and addresses public health preparedness
item.description: Kerala Devaswoms Minister K. Muraleedharan has stated that strict action will be taken against all those found responsible in the Sabarimala gold loss case, irrespective of their political affiliation...

item.title: Zanzibar private sector highlights strengths and gaps in 2026/27 budget draft and calls for broader business involvement
item.description: Private sector stakeholders in Zanzibar have reviewed the 2026/27 draft budget of the Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar (SMZ), noting both positive elements and key gaps that need attention. The pr...

item.title: 1851 Daguerreotype Documents the Life of Caesar, an Enslaved Man in New York
item.description: The article examines the historical significance of an 1851 daguerreotype portrait of Caesar, an enslaved African American man who lived in New York during the 18th and 19th centuries. The image is co...

item.title: 11 terrorists killed in intelligence-based operation in North Waziristan, says ISPR
item.description: According to the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), security forces conducted a series of intelligence-based operations in the Datta Khel area of North Waziristan district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, ...

item.title: Grizz Chapman, ‘30 Rock’ actor, dies at 52 following illness
item.description: Grizz Chapman, best known for his role in the NBC sitcom ‘30 Rock’, has died at the age of 52. His passing was confirmed by his representative, Saideh A Brown, on Friday, May 22, 2026. While no offici...

item.title: White House Communications Director Sparks Backlash After Profane Response to Mike Pompeo Criticism
item.description: White House Communications Director Steven Cheung drew widespread criticism after issuing a profanity-laced response to former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo. The dispute began when Pompeo publicly cr...

item.title: Pakistan and 13 countries reject Somaliland’s plan to open embassy in occupied Jerusalem
item.description: Pakistan, along with 13 other countries, has strongly condemned the announcement by the self-declared region of Somaliland regarding the opening of a so-called embassy in occupied Jerusalem. In a join...

item.title: Tinubu secures sweeping victory in APC presidential primary in Ondo State
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has recorded a decisive win in the All Progressives Congress (APC) presidential primary held in Ondo State, where he reportedly secured an overwhelming number of votes. The exerc...

item.title: Police in Kisumu destroy over 1,000 litres of illicit alcohol in Muhoroni crackdown
item.description: Police officers in Kisumu County have carried out a successful crackdown on illegal alcohol production, destroying more than 1,000 litres of illicit brew commonly known as kangara in Muhoroni. The ope...

item.title: A comprehensive tax reform strategy aimed at improving Canada’s economic growth
item.description: The editorial examines Canada’s current fiscal and taxation landscape and argues that the country does not face a revenue shortage, but rather a structural imbalance in how tax revenues are collected....

item.title: Local leaders and residents in Basilan take oath as members of Bangsamoro Federalist Party ahead of BARMM elections
item.description: Hundreds of Muslim and Christian community leaders in Basilan formally joined the Bangsamoro Federalist Party during a mass oathtaking ceremony held in Lamitan City on May 23. The gathering brought to...

item.title: Fadnavis dismisses Rahul Gandhi’s prediction of NDA collapse, calls it unrealistic
item.description: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis has dismissed remarks attributed to Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi, in which he reportedly suggested that the NDA government could collapse within a yea...

item.title: Gunila and the Elegance of Mid-Century High Fashion Photography
item.description: Gunila was a prominent fashion model of the 1950s and 1960s, widely recognized for embodying the refined “Nordic look” that captivated the international fashion industry. With her sharp cheekbones, ex...
